task_completion.description: OpenBCI GUI application is launched and visible
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI application window is open and visible on the desktop.

task_completion.description: Data source is configured to SYNTHETIC
task_completion.visual_evidence: The system control panel or data source selection area in the GUI indicates 'Synthetic' is selected.

task_completion.description: The data stream has been started
task_completion.visual_evidence: Active waveforms are being drawn in the GUI, and the main control button displays 'Stop Data Stream' (which indicates the stream is currently running).

task_completion.description: All 16 channels are active and visible in the Time Series widget
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Time Series widget displays exactly 16 distinct channel rows (typically labeled 1 through 16) with active synthetic waveforms.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the running session is saved to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or image viewer confirms the existence of a file named '16ch_synthetic_session.png' in the '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI is running as a native application window, not being viewed inside a web browser or an image viewer.

integrity.description: The saved screenshot is a genuine image file
integrity.visual_evidence: The file '16ch_synthetic_session.png' appears as a valid image (e.g., showing a thumbnail or successfully opened in an image viewer), not a text file renamed with a .png extension.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply download or copy a pre-existing image
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of a web browser open to a search for OpenBCI screenshots, and the screenshot matches the actual GUI state configured by the agent.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected screenshot path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/16ch_synthetic_session.png'. The channel count must be 16, which corresponds to the Cyton+Daisy board configuration. The data source must be 'SYNTHETIC', which generates algorithmic test waveforms.
